checks.name: 1.1 bump-version
checks.description: Bump version atomically with bump2version — update X.Y.Z-RC-N to the actual release version before running

checks.name: 1.2 update-roadmap
checks.description: Set release Completion=100% and Status=Closed in docs/docs/architecture/roadmap.md; move incomplete issues to next milestone
cmd: echo 'Open docs/docs/architecture/roadmap.md and update milestone status'

checks.name: 1.3.1 check-dependabot-alerts
checks.description: Check for open critical Dependabot alerts — prints count, instructs you to fix any before proceeding
cmd: count=$(gh api repos/IBM/mcp-context-forge/dependabot/alerts --jq '[.[] | select(.state=="open" and .security_advisory.severity == "critical")] | length'); if [ "$count" -gt 0 ]; then echo ""; echo "⚠️  WARNING: $count open critical Dependabot alert(s) found."; echo "   Resolve them at: https://github.com/IBM/mcp-context-forge/security/dependabot"; echo "   Do NOT proceed until all critical alerts are dismissed or patched."; echo ""; else echo "✅ OK: no open critical Dependabot alerts — safe to proceed."; fi

checks.name: 1.3.2 check-code-scanning-alerts
checks.description: Check for open code scanning alerts — prints count, instructs you to review any before proceeding
cmd: count=$(gh api repos/IBM/mcp-context-forge/code-scanning/alerts --jq '[.[] | select(.state=="open")] | length'); if [ "$count" -gt 0 ]; then echo ""; echo "⚠️  WARNING: $count open code scanning alert(s) found."; echo "   Review them at: https://github.com/IBM/mcp-context-forge/security/code-scanning"; echo "   Resolve or triage each one before tagging the release."; echo ""; else echo "✅ OK: no open code scanning alerts — safe to proceed."; fi

checks.name: 1.4.1 check-base-images
checks.description: Print current FROM lines in Containerfile — manually update each tag to the latest before running 1.4.2

checks.name: 1.4.2 build-no-cache
checks.description: Rebuild production container after base-image bump to verify new tags build cleanly

checks.name: 2.1 update-cpex-deps
checks.description: Update CPEX exclude-newer date in pyproject.toml manually, then run uv lock --upgrade

checks.name: 2.2.1 update-deps-all-pyproject
checks.description: Run uv-upsync + uv lock --upgrade for every pyproject.toml — fails if any project fails to lock
cmd: uv tool install uv-upsync; find . -path './mcp-servers/templates' -prune -o -path '*/.venv' -prune -o -path '*/target' -prune -o -path './.cache' -prune -o -name 'pyproject.toml' -type f -print | while read -r toml_file; do dir=$(dirname "$toml_file"); echo "==> $dir"; uv-upsync --project "$dir" 2>/dev/null || true; uv lock --upgrade --exclude-newer '10 days' --project "$dir" || { echo "FAIL: uv lock failed in $dir"; exit 1; }; done

checks.name: 2.2.2 update-deps-requirements-txt
checks.description: Update all requirements.txt files via the GitHub tools updater — fails if any file fails
cmd: find . -path '*/.venv' -prune -o -path './.cache' -prune -o -name 'requirements.txt' -type f -print | while read -r req_file; do echo "==> $req_file"; uv run .github/tools/update_dependencies.py --file "$req_file" || { echo "FAIL: update failed for $req_file"; exit 1; }; done

checks.name: 2.3 install-dev
checks.description: Reinstall dev environment after dependency updates

checks.name: 2.4.1 pip-audit
checks.description: Audit dependencies for known CVEs — fails if any CVE found
cmd: source .venv/bin/activate && uv pip install -q pip-audit && pip-audit --strict

checks.name: 2.4.2 rebuild-and-test
checks.description: Rebuild production container with updated deps and run the full test suite

checks.name: 3.1.1 cargo-update
checks.description: Update Cargo.lock for the whole workspace

checks.name: 3.1.2 rust-check
checks.description: Verify Rust build, lint, and tests (cargo fmt --check + clippy -D warnings + cargo test)

checks.name: 3.2.1 rust-vet
checks.description: Supply-chain vetting with cargo-vet — fails if any unvetted crate found

checks.name: 3.2.2 cargo-vet-prune
checks.description: Prune stale cargo-vet exemptions after vetting

checks.name: 3.3.1 npm-update
checks.description: Update npm packages and fix audit issues
cmd: npm update && npm audit && npm audit fix

checks.name: 3.3.2 lint-web
checks.description: Run frontend linting

checks.name: 3.3.3 test-js-coverage
checks.description: Run JavaScript unit tests with coverage (Vitest + Istanbul)

checks.name: 3.4.0 build-ui
checks.description: Rebuild Admin UI Vite bundle after npm dependency updates

checks.name: 3.4.1 rebuild-containers
checks.description: Rebuild production container from scratch after all dependency ecosystem updates

checks.name: 3.4.2 smoke-test-admin-ui
checks.description: Manually verify Admin UI bundled assets load correctly in a browser after bundle rebuild
cmd: make compose-up && echo 'Open http://localhost:8080/admin/ and verify the UI loads with no missing assets'

checks.name: 3.4.3 make compose down
checks.description: Stop test server

checks.name: 4.1 verify
checks.description: Validate wheel and sdist with twine, check-manifest, and pyroma

checks.name: 4.2 detect-secrets-scan
checks.description: Full-tree secrets scan — fails if any unaudited secrets found in the baseline
cmd: make detect-secrets-scan; unaudited=$(uv tool run detect-secrets audit --report .secrets.baseline 2>/dev/null | grep -c 'Unaudited' || true); if [ "$unaudited" -gt 0 ]; then echo "FAIL: $unaudited unaudited secret(s) — run make detect-secrets-audit to triage"; exit 1; fi; echo 'OK: no unaudited secrets'

checks.name: 5.1 coverage
checks.description: Python unit tests with coverage report — review for significant regressions

checks.name: 5.2 test-js-coverage
checks.description: JavaScript unit tests with Istanbul coverage (Vitest)

checks.name: 5.3 test-ui-headless
checks.description: Playwright UI tests in headless Chromium — requires compose stack from section 6

checks.name: 5.4.0 start testing stack
checks.description: Start the Testing Stack
cmd: make testing-down compose-clean testing-up && i=1; while [ $i -le 30 ]; do if curl -sf http://localhost:8080/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'OK: gateway healthy'; break; fi; echo "Waiting for gateway... ($i/30)"; sleep 2; if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then echo 'FAIL: gateway did not become healthy after 60s'; exit 1; fi; i=$((i+1)); done

checks.name: 5.4.1 test-mcp-rbac
checks.description: RBAC enforcement and multi-transport MCP protocol compliance tests — requires compose stack

checks.name: 5.4.2 test-mcp-protocol-e2e
checks.description: MCP protocol end-to-end tests via FastMCP client — requires compose stack

checks.name: 5.5.1 load-test-cli
checks.description: 10-minute sustained load test — acceptance gate: 1000 RPS target, 0% error rate

checks.name: 5.5.2 load-test-light
checks.description: Quick smoke load test: 10 users, 30s

checks.name: 5.5.3 load-test-heavy
checks.description: Stress load test: 200 users, 120s

checks.name: 5.5.4 load-test-sustained
checks.description: Endurance load test: 25 users, 300s

checks.name: 5.5.5 load-test-stress
checks.description: Peak load test: 500 users, 60s

checks.name: 5.6.0 clean  testing stack
checks.description: Clean the Testing Stack

checks.name: 6.1 docker-prod
checks.description: Build production container from scratch

checks.name: 6.2.1 compose-stack-up
checks.description: Bring up full testing compose stack — waits up to 60s for gateway health before continuing
cmd: make testing-down compose-clean testing-up && i=1; while [ $i -le 30 ]; do if curl -sf http://localhost:8080/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'OK: gateway healthy'; break; fi; echo "Waiting for gateway... ($i/30)"; sleep 2; if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then echo 'FAIL: gateway did not become healthy after 60s'; exit 1; fi; i=$((i+1)); done

checks.name: 6.2.2 compose-ps
checks.description: Verify all compose services are running or healthy — fails with a list of bad containers if any are not
cmd: make compose-ps; bad=$(docker compose ps --format '{{.Name}}	{{.State}}	{{.Health}}' | awk 'NR>0 && $2!="running" && $2!="healthy" {print $1" ("$2")" }'); if [ -n "$bad" ]; then echo "FAIL: unhealthy services: $bad"; exit 1; fi; echo 'OK: all services running/healthy'

checks.name: 6.3.1 embedded-up
checks.description: Start embedded/iframe mode with benchmark servers

checks.name: 6.3.2 embedded-smoke-test
checks.description: Manually verify Admin UI renders in iframe at http://localhost:8889 and benchmark tools appear in catalog
cmd: echo 'Open http://localhost:8889 and verify iframe UI, tool catalog, and navigation'

checks.name: 6.3.3 embedded-down
checks.description: Tear down embedded mode stack

checks.name: 6.4.1 dist
checks.description: Build wheel and sdist

checks.name: 6.4.2 verify
checks.description: Validate built packages with twine, check-manifest, pyroma

checks.name: 7.1 compose-sso
checks.description: Start full stack with Keycloak SSO profile (gateway :8080, Keycloak :8180 admin/changeme)

checks.name: 7.2 sso-login-flow
checks.description: Manually verify SSO login: navigate to /admin/, confirm Keycloak redirect, log in, verify session and RBAC roles, test logout
cmd: echo 'Open http://localhost:8080/admin/ and complete the SSO login verification checklist'

checks.name: 7.3 compose-sso-down
checks.description: Tear down SSO compose stack

checks.name: 8.1 monitoring-up
checks.description: Start monitoring stack — verifies Grafana (:3000) and Prometheus (:9090) are reachable before continuing
cmd: make monitoring-up && i=1; while [ $i -le 15 ]; do if curl -sf http://localhost:3000/api/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'OK: Grafana healthy'; break; fi; echo "Waiting for Grafana... ($i/15)"; sleep 2; if [ $i -eq 15 ]; then echo 'FAIL: Grafana did not become healthy after 30s'; exit 1; fi; i=$((i+1)); done && curl -sf http://localhost:9090/-/healthy >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo 'OK: Prometheus healthy' || { echo 'FAIL: Prometheus not healthy'; exit 1; }

checks.name: 8.2 load-test-with-monitoring
checks.description: Run load test while monitoring stack is active — then verify all Prometheus targets are UP
cmd: make load-test-cli && down=$(curl -sf 'http://localhost:9090/api/v1/targets' | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); bad=[t['labels'].get('job','?') for t in d['data']['activeTargets'] if t['health']!='up']; print('\n'.join(bad))"); if [ -n "$down" ]; then echo "FAIL: Prometheus targets not UP: $down"; exit 1; fi; echo 'OK: all Prometheus targets UP'

checks.name: 8.3 grafana-dashboard-check
checks.description: Manually verify Grafana: MCP Gateway Overview dashboard shows live metrics, latency graphs render, Tempo traces visible
cmd: echo 'Open http://localhost:3000 (admin/changeme) and verify the MCP Gateway Overview dashboard'

checks.name: 8.4 monitoring-down
checks.description: Tear down monitoring stack

checks.name: 9.1.1 sonar-up
checks.description: Start SonarQube server and wait until fully ready (up to 120s)
cmd: make sonar-up-docker && i=1; while [ $i -le 24 ]; do status=$(curl -sf http://localhost:9000/api/system/status 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('status',''))" 2>/dev/null); if [ "$status" = 'UP' ]; then echo 'OK: SonarQube is UP'; break; fi; echo "Waiting for SonarQube... ($i/24) status=$status"; sleep 5; if [ $i -eq 24 ]; then echo 'FAIL: SonarQube did not reach UP state after 120s'; exit 1; fi; i=$((i+1)); done

checks.name: 9.1.2 sonar-submit
checks.description: Submit SonarQube scan

checks.name: 9.1.3 sonar-review
checks.description: Manually review SonarQube report at http://localhost:9000 — acceptance: no new bugs, vulnerabilities, or security hotspots
cmd: echo 'Open http://localhost:9000 and review the scan results'

checks.name: 9.2 semgrep
checks.description: Run Semgrep security analysis — fails if any findings reported (bypasses the || true in the Makefile)

checks.name: 9.3 sbom
checks.description: Generate CycloneDX SBOM (mcpgateway.sbom.xml) for the release artifact

checks.name: 9.4 container-scan
checks.description: Verify the latest docker-scan.yml CI run completed successfully — fails if last run was not success
cmd: conclusion=$(gh run list --workflow=docker-scan.yml --limit=1 --json conclusion --jq '.[0].conclusion'); echo "docker-scan.yml last run: $conclusion"; if [ "$conclusion" != 'success' ]; then echo "FAIL: last docker-scan.yml run was '$conclusion' — check GitHub Actions"; exit 1; fi; echo 'OK: docker-scan.yml passed'

checks.name: 10.1 markdown-links
checks.description: Scan Markdown files for broken internal and external links

checks.name: 10.2 docs-review
checks.description: Run local MkDocs server and manually review: new content, navigation, code blocks, CHANGELOG and roadmap pages

checks.name: 10.3.1 mike-deploy
checks.description: Deploy versioned docs with mike — update VERSION=X.Y.Z before running

checks.name: 10.3.2 mike-set-default
checks.description: Set the deployed version as the default landing page

checks.name: 10.3.3 mike-list
checks.description: List all deployed documentation versions to verify the deployment succeeded

checks.description: Run all four upgrade scenarios (SQLite/PostgreSQL × fresh/upgrade) via automated harness

checks.name: 11.2 manual-compose-upgrade-postgres

cmd: echo 'See release-manual-steps.md §14 for the full PostgreSQL compose upgrade procedure'

checks.name: 11.3.1 migration-test-all
checks.description: Run comprehensive migration test suite (sequential upgrades, downgrades, skip-version)

checks.name: 11.3.2 migration-test-performance
checks.description: Run migration performance tests

checks.name: 12.0.1 init-secrets
checks.description: Ensure JWT_SECRET_KEY is set in .env (not the placeholder) — generate if needed
cmd: make init-secrets && grep JWT_SECRET_KEY .env | grep -v '__REPLACE_ME__' && echo 'OK: JWT_SECRET_KEY is set' || { echo 'FAIL: JWT_SECRET_KEY still contains placeholder'; exit 1; }

checks.name: 12.0.2 stack-up
checks.description: Build image and start compose stack for manual testing

checks.name: 12.1 generate-jwt
checks.description: Generate a JWT bearer token for manual API calls
cmd: export JWT_SECRET_KEY=$(grep '^JWT_SECRET_KEY=' .env | cut -d= -f2-) && python -m mcpgateway.utils.create_jwt_token --username admin@example.com --exp 10080 --secret "$JWT_SECRET_KEY"

checks.name: 12.2 register-sse-gateway
checks.description: Start mcp-server-time via SSE on :8002 and register with gateway — manually verify tools appear in catalog
cmd: echo 'See release-manual-steps.md §18 for translate + curl commands'

checks.name: 12.3 register-streamable-http-gateway
checks.description: Start mcp-server-time via Streamable HTTP on :8003 and register — manually verify tools in catalog
cmd: echo 'See release-manual-steps.md §19 for translate + curl commands'

checks.name: 12.4 create-virtual-server-and-export
checks.description: Create virtual server wrapping both gateways and export config — manually verify export contains both gateways and server
cmd: echo 'See release-manual-steps.md §20 for curl + cli export commands'

checks.name: 12.5 mcp-inspector
checks.description: Launch MCP Inspector and manually verify SSE + Streamable HTTP transports against the virtual server

checks.name: 12.6 vscode-copilot
checks.description: Configure .vscode/mcp.json and manually verify GitHub Copilot tool calls work with both SSE and Streamable HTTP

checks.name: 12.7 cleanup
checks.description: Stop background translate processes and remove test export file

checks.description: Add a new section to CHANGELOG.md: '## [X.Y.Z] - YYYY-MM-DD - Release Title' with Added/Changed/Fixed/Security entries
